guideline_references: Vascular guidance on acute limb/digital ischaemia
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: severe cold-triggered digital ischaemia (Raynaud's) with arthralgia mimics acute arterial embolism and evolving systemic sclerosis.
Important-not-to-miss: critical/irreversible digital ischaemia and arterial embolism must be safety-netted.
Anchoring: 'known UCTD Raynaud's' should not close off critical ischaemia if a digit stays white or turns black.
Reverse-triage nuance: a typical rewarming Raynaud's attack should not trigger a reflexive ambulance, but a persistently white/black digit means ED.
Telehealth limits: pulses/perfusion and joint examination cannot be assessed over chat, though the patient can self-observe finger colour and trial rewarming.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
